branch_point_type: simple-resurgent algebraic-logarithmic branch point; expected square-root-class component, but exponent must be fitted from large-order data
stokes_coefficient_extraction_recipe: Use the exact action A = zeta_star = 4/sqrt(3). Fix the Birkhoff/Borel normalization and factorial shift. Estimate the branch exponent beta from high-order coefficient ratios A*a[n+1]/a[n]-n, including log corrections if needed. Form R_n = a_n*A^(n+beta)/Gamma(n+beta), fit its inverse-power asymptotic tail over high-order windows, and accelerate R_n to n=infinity by Richardson/Weniger transforms. Convert the limiting amplitude C to the Stokes coefficient by S_A = 2*pi*i*C/c0_A, where c0_A is the chosen leading normalization of the adjacent alien sector. Cross-check by local Borel singular-germ fitting near zeta_star and by verifying the lateral discontinuity/median half-jump.
predicted_closed_form_candidates: rational powers of 2, 3, and pi times Gamma(1/3), Gamma(2/3), Gamma(1/6), Gamma(5/6)
elementary sine/cosine/gamma monodromy constants from the Painleve-III(D6) Riemann-Hilbert normalization
zeta-values in fluctuation coefficients rather than necessarily in the leading Stokes constant
Catalan constant or Gamma(1/4) only if an independent square-lattice/tau=i normalization appears
isomonodromic_correspondence_caveat: True conditionally: the extracted Stokes coefficient must match P-III(D6) Stokes/monodromy data under the same normalization; extraction alone is not the proof.
estimated_compute_cost: No new coefficients required. Theory recipe uses existing 5000 coefficients at 250 dps plus exact zeta_star. Expected offline cost is minutes to hours for high-precision sequence acceleration, local singular-germ checks, and PSLQ trials; avoid brute-force global Pade-Borel-Laplace as primary estimator.
failure_modes: wrong factorial shift or coefficient normalization
unmodeled logarithmic companion at the branch point
nearby secondary Borel singularity of comparable modulus
mixed conjugate actions or unresolved Stokes direction conventions
insufficient precision in late coefficients after normalization
